title: SEC-5: Validate SIWE message domain
description:
Verify SIWE message domain validation in verifySiweMessage():

```typescript
const expectedDomain = process.env.NEXTAUTH_URL
  ? new URL(process.env.NEXTAUTH_URL).host
  : "localhost:3000"
if (siweMessage.domain !== expectedDomain) {
  throw new Error("Domain mismatch")
}
```

**Why:** Prevents using a SIWE message signed for a different website. An attacker could ask a user to sign a SIWE message on evil.com and replay it on our app. Domain validation prevents this.

**Acceptance:** SIWE messages with wrong domain are rejected.

title: SEC-6: Validate SIWE message expiration
description:
Verify SIWE message expiration validation in verifySiweMessage():

```typescript
if (siweMessage.expirationTime && new Date(siweMessage.expirationTime) < new Date()) {
  throw new Error("Message expired")
}
```

Combined with the 5-minute nonce cookie TTL, this provides defense in depth. Even if a nonce cookie somehow persists, the SIWE message itself has an expiration time set at signing (5 minutes from issuedAt).

**Acceptance:** Expired SIWE messages are rejected even if nonce is valid.

description:
**File:** `lib/auth.ts` — inside the SIWE authorize callback (part of 1C-2)

The User find-or-create uses Prisma upsert on walletAddress:

```typescript
const address = checksumAddress(verifiedAddress)  // always checksum!

const user = await prisma.user.upsert({
  where: { walletAddress: address },
  update: { ensName },  // update cached ENS on re-login
  create: {
    walletAddress: address,
    ensName,
```

**Key details:**

- Always use checksummed address to avoid case-sensitivity duplicates

- On re-login, update the cached `ensName` (it may have changed on-chain)

title: 2A-3: Implement parallel ENS text record resolution
description:
**File:** `lib/web3/ens-service.ts` (part of resolveEnsProfile)

Use `Promise.allSettled()` (not `Promise.all()`) for parallel resolution of ENS text records. This ensures:
- If one text record fails (e.g., RPC rate limit on one call), others still succeed
- Each rejected promise maps to `null` — graceful degradation
- Total resolution time is max(individual calls) not sum(individual calls)

```typescript
const results = await Promise.allSettled([
  client.getEnsText({ name, key: "avatar" }),
  client.getEnsText({ name, key: "description" }),
  // ...etc
])

// Map: fulfilled → value, rejected → null
const getValue = (r: PromiseSettledResult<string | null>) =>
  r.status === "fulfilled" ? r.value ?? null : null
```

**Why not Promise.all?** A single RPC failure would reject the entire batch, losing all other successfully resolved records.

**Acceptance:** If one text record fails, others still return. No unhandled rejections.
close_reason: Promise.allSettled for parallel ENS text records already in resolveEnsProfile

title: SEC-3: Set httpOnly flag on nonce cookie
description:
Verify nonce cookie security attributes:

In `generateNonce()` (lib/web3/siwe.ts), the cookie must have:
- `httpOnly: true` — not accessible via document.cookie (prevents XSS-based theft)
- `sameSite: "strict"` — not sent on cross-origin requests (prevents CSRF)
- `secure: true` (in production) — only sent over HTTPS
- `maxAge: 300` — 5-minute TTL
- `path: "/"` — available to all routes

**Acceptance:** Cookie attributes verified via browser dev tools. All security flags set correctly.

```typescript
import { getAddress } from "viem"

export function checksumAddress(address: string): string {
  return getAddress(address)  // Returns EIP-55 checksummed address
}
```

This utility MUST be called at every boundary where an Ethereum address enters the system:
- SIWE authorize callback (before DB upsert)
- ENS resolution service (resolved addresses)
- Self Protocol verify-pass (proof address)

`viem.getAddress()` throws if the input is not a valid Ethereum address, which also serves as input validation.

title: 2E-4: Add blockie/gradient avatar to AddressDisplay
description:
**File:** `components/address-display.tsx`

Add a deterministic visual avatar when no ENS avatar is available. Options:
1. **Gradient based on address** — simplest, use address bytes to generate CSS gradient
2. **Blockie** — classic Ethereum blockie pattern (requires a small library or manual implementation)

Simplest approach for hackathon:
```typescript
function addressGradient(address: string): string {
  const hash = address.slice(2, 14)  // first 6 bytes
  const color1 = \`#\${hash.slice(0, 6)}\`
  const color2 = \`#\${hash.slice(6, 12)}\`
  return \`linear-gradient(135deg, \${color1}, \${color2})\`
}

<div
  className="h-6 w-6 rounded-full"
  style={{ background: addressGradient(address) }}
/>
```

**Priority:** P3 — only if time permits. AddressDisplay works fine without it.

**Acceptance:** A colored circle appears next to the address, deterministic per address.
close_reason: Gradient avatar based on address bytes added to AddressDisplay

title: 3C-2: Implement legacy floor passthrough
description:
**File:** `lib/floor-access.ts` (part of requireFloorAccess)

Legacy floor passthrough logic:

```typescript
if (!floor?.requiredSelfPassId) return
```

When `requiredSelfPassId` is null (all floors except floor-12), the function returns immediately without checking membership. This ensures existing floor behavior is completely unchanged.

**Why this matters:** Floors 1-11 and 14-16 have no gating. The existing auth (requireEitherAuth/requireEitherMember) on their routes is sufficient. Adding requireFloorAccess to their routes must be a no-op for these floors.

**Acceptance:** All existing floor routes continue to work identically for non-gated floors.

title: 1E-7: Construct SIWE message with correct fields
description:
**File:** `components/wallet-connect-button.tsx` (or a helper in `lib/web3/siwe-message.ts`)

Construct a SIWE message string with correct fields per EIP-4361:

```typescript
function createSiweMessage(address: string, nonce: string): string {
  const domain = window.location.host
  const origin = window.location.origin
  const now = new Date()
  const expiration = new Date(now.getTime() + 5 * 60 * 1000)  // 5 min from now

  return [
    \`\${domain} wants you to sign in with your Ethereum account:\`,
    address,
    "",
    "Sign in to Frontier Atlas",
    "",
    \`URI: \${origin}\`,
    "Version: 1",
    "Chain ID: 1",
    \`Nonce: \${nonce}\`,
    \`Issued At: \${now.toISOString()}\`,
    \`Expiration Time: \${expiration.toISOString()}\`,
  ].join("\\n")
}
```

**Note:** This is the raw string format. The server parses it with the `siwe` library's `SiweMessage` class.

**Acceptance:** Generated message is valid SIWE format. Server-side `new SiweMessage(message)` parses it without errors.

title: 3B-5: Ensure Self proof is idempotent
description:
**File:** `app/api/floors/[floorId]/verify-pass/route.ts`

Ensure idempotency via upsert operations:

- `memberFloorMembership.upsert` with `memberId_floorId` composite key: if membership exists, update status to ACTIVE; if not, create
- `memberProfile.upsert` with `memberId` key: if profile exists, no-op (empty update); if not, create draft

Re-verification should NOT:
- Create duplicate MemberFloorMembership rows
- Reset an existing published profile to draft
- Change homeFloorId if user already has a profile

**Acceptance:** Calling verify-pass twice for same user+floor produces identical database state. No duplicate rows.

title: 1C-4: Switch session strategy to JWT-only
description:
**File:** `lib/auth.ts`

Change session strategy from conditional to always JWT:

```typescript
// BEFORE (line 86):
session: { strategy: isDev ? "jwt" : "database" },

// AFTER:
session: { strategy: "jwt" },
```

**Why:** NextAuth v5 Credentials providers require JWT strategy. Since SIWE is a Credentials provider available in both dev and prod, we need JWT everywhere.

**Side effects:**
1. Existing database sessions are invalidated — all users must re-sign-in (acceptable for hackathon)
2. The PrismaAdapter is still needed for Nodemailer's email verification flow (account linking), so do NOT remove it
3. The session callback must also change to always use `token.id` instead of conditionally using `user.id` (see 1C-7)

**Acceptance:** `session.strategy` is `"jwt"` unconditionally. Server starts without errors.

title: 2C-2: Handle address vs name input in ENS endpoint
description:
**File:** `app/api/ens/[addressOrName]/route.ts` (part of 2C-1)

Handle both address and ENS name inputs in the endpoint:

**Address detection logic:**
- Starts with `0x` AND length is 42 → treat as Ethereum address, checksum it
- Otherwise → treat as ENS name (e.g., "vitalik.eth"), forward-resolve to get address

**Edge cases:**
- Invalid address (0x but wrong length) → viem.getAddress() throws, caught by formatApiError → 500
- Non-existent ENS name → resolveEnsAddress returns null → 404
- Address with no ENS records → returns EnsProfile with all null fields (not an error)

**Acceptance:** Both `vitalik.eth` and `0xd8dA...` inputs work. Invalid inputs return appropriate errors.
close_reason: Address vs name handling already implemented in ENS endpoint
